notes: Game needs native d3dx9_43 and d3dcompiler_43, otherwise it will crash.
title: Sonic CD

notes:
after setting up dx9 and the missing directx 43 file. the game ran almost flawlessly, i did notice a nasty hiccup on the first level near the very start, not sure what caused it. but after that,  i played through to the second zone.
I did use my ps3 controller without any issues, but i've borked the 360 controller emulation on my system, so rocket league and gta5 won't use it anymore.
title: Sonic CD

notes: As the other reports say, you need to use protontricks or winetricks, and install d3dcompiler_43 and d3dx9 to get the game running. After that its smooth as butter.
title: Sonic CD

notes: It was necessary to install, on game folder, the DLLs "D3DCompiler_43.dll" and "D3DX9_43.dll" in 32 bits version. The Joypad is not working, so I set the controller as keyboard on Controller Configuration. "Video Setup" is not working, the game runs in highest resolution possible.
title: Sonic CD

notes: The game itself works great after copying D3DCompiler_43.dll and D3DX9_43.dll into the game's directory (obtained 32 bit versions from www.dll-files.com), and setting up libstrangle to limit the framerate to 60 by installing it using the instructions from the libstrangle GitLab page, then using the launch option "strangle 60 %command%" (without quotes). Tested using Steam controller. Checked all filter options, all work as expected.  The video settings dialog does not work, however all it does is let you select your resolution but the game defaults to your maximum resolution by default so as long as you have a high-end machine you should be fine.
title: Sonic CD

notes: As others have pointed out, use either dll-files.com or winetricks to download the 32-bit versions of D3DCompiler_43.dll and D3DX9_43.dll and put them in the directory that Sonic CD was installed to. The game seems to run perfectly after that.
title: Sonic CD

notes: Runs perfectly, but only after running 'WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/200940/pfx winetricks d3dcompiler_43 d3dx9' in terminal. The changing the video filter in the Settings menu has no effect meaning you are stuck using the 'smooth' option. Otherwise works fine.
title: Sonic CD

notes: Ran perfectly after installing the game and running the command "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/200940/pfx winetricks d3dcompiler_43 d3dx9" from a terminal (Fedora downloaded all Wine packages for me too after running this command!) Game ran perfectly on this ancient hardware.
title: Sonic CD

notes: Ran protontricks 200940 d3dcompiler_43 d3dx9 first. Game does run too fast to be fully playable, but it can be done.
title: Sonic CD

notes: run   WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/200940/pfx" wine "$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Sonic CD/directx/DXSETUP.exe"  to install bundled directx
title: Sonic CD

notes:
I ran the commands: 
WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/200940/pfx"

and

wine "$HOME/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Sonic CD/directx/DXSETUP.exe"

and this alone did not work, so at the suggestion at other posters here I downloaded the 32-bit versions of D3DCompiler_43.dll and D3DX9_43.dll and put them in the root of the /steamapps/common/Sonic CD/ folder, and this let the game run. 

However, because I have a 144 Hz monitor, the game was sped up. Setting my display to run at 60 Hz solved this issue and let the game run correctly. 

I was also getting screen tearing, which appears to be caused by XFCE (I'm using Xubuntu). Disabling XFCE's display composition using Window Manager Tweaks solved this.
title: Sonic CD
